_meta.purpose: Sword-swap variant — take the L1_Base infantryman and replace the spiked wooden mace with a basic levy arming sword. Output: a new L1 base with sword for an alternate progression chain.
history: v1 (2026-05-06): initial author — single-edit weapon swap (spiked wooden mace → basic levy arming sword) on the L1_Base. Sword shape: single-handed straight cross-guard arming sword, wooden grip wrap, simple steel blade, modest cross-guard, plain pommel. Pose preserved: held in same anatomical-RIGHT hand, blade angled down toward viewer-LEFT to fill the same silhouette envelope as the mace did. All other elements (mail coif, livery tunic, leather bracers, blue trousers, boots, round wooden shield, beard, face, framing) preserved exactly. Output is intended as a new L1 base (`Refs/L1_Sword_Base.png` after keeper selection) for a parallel sword-progression chain.

v6 (2026-05-07): scrub model-invisible context from prompt body — removed L<n> level-tag references (model is stateless and cannot resolve our level naming), authoring-decision parentheticals (LOCKED/REVISED/REMOVED dates, per AD FeedbackV0, etc.), and cross-level range refs. All persistent decisions remain in _meta.history + the progression plan + CLAUDE.md. See Archer/CLAUDE.md § Part 3 locked decision #27.

prompt: EDIT GOAL — Replace the spiked wooden mace held in the infantryman's anatomical-RIGHT hand with a BASIC LEVY ARMING SWORD. The mace is FULLY REMOVED — no wooden haft, no bulbous wooden head, no iron spikes anywhere in the new image. The new sword is held in the same hand, in the same hand-position, with the blade angled down and toward the viewer-LEFT (mirroring the mace head's previous position) so the overall silhouette envelope of the weapon is similar to what the mace occupied. SWORD DESCRIPTION: a simple single-handed straight arming sword, approximately the same overall length as the previous mace. The sword has: (1) a STRAIGHT DOUBLE-EDGED STEEL BLADE, cool silver-gray polished steel, with a subtle vertical fuller (shallow groove) running down the center of the blade, tapering smoothly from the cross-guard to a sharp point at the tip; (2) a SIMPLE STRAIGHT STEEL CROSS-GUARD perpendicular to the blade, approximately 4 inches wide tip-to-tip, dark cool-gray steel, with rounded ends; (3) a WARM-BROWN LEATHER GRIP WRAP covering the entire grip section between the cross-guard and the pommel, diagonal-wrapped leather cord with visible texture, matching the warm-brown leather of the existing forearm bracers and belt; (4) a PLAIN ROUND STEEL POMMEL at the bottom of the grip, small disc-shaped polished steel pommel approximately 1.5 inches in diameter. The hand grips the leather wrap of the sword's grip; the cross-guard sits just above the hand at the level of the upper coif as the mace head did previously; the blade extends downward and toward the viewer-LEFT, with the blade's tip near hip height angled down. ===== PRESERVE FROM THE INPUT IMAGE EXACTLY ===== every other element of the input image must remain identical in shape, color, position, and proportion: his chiseled chibi jawline; his stern flat closed-mouth expression with no smile and no snarl; his thick dark-brown eyebrows with a slight stern furrow; his large warm steel-blue eyes with a single bright catchlight in each iris and visible whites of the eyes; his prominent slightly-hooked nose; his warm peachy-tan skin tone with a soft rosy blush on the cheeks above the beard line; his FULL SHORT-TRIMMED DARK-BROWN BEARD covering the chin and lower cheeks reaching the upper neck — trimmed short on the cheeks, NOT bushy, NOT wild, NOT long; his dark mustache continuous with the beard; his dark-brown short hair visible only as a thin fringe at the forehead under the front edge of the mail coif; the chunky scale-mail coif covering the entire scalp, ears, and neck down to the collarbone, with chunky overlapping rounded scale-shaped plates in cool silver-gray steel arranged in horizontal rows where each row overlaps the row below by half a scale; the sleeveless mustard-yellow and crimson-red split LIVERY TUNIC — viewer-LEFT half solid crimson-red, viewer-RIGHT half mustard-yellow with a vertical crimson-red center stripe running collar to hem, simple round neckline, hem reaches mid-thigh; the twisted-rope cord belt at the waist with simple knot at the front; both bare athletic biceps exposed from the shoulder down; the existing brown leather forearm bracers wrapping wrist to mid-forearm on both arms with single visible cross-strap binding per bracer; the bare warm peachy-tan skin of the hand gripping the new sword (anatomical-RIGHT hand, viewer-LEFT side of frame); the round wooden buckler-style shield held in the anatomical-LEFT arm (viewer-RIGHT side of frame) — approximately 24 inches in diameter, warm-brown wooden plank face with faint vertical wood grain, central cool-gray steel boss as a small dome, plain rim with no rivets, no painted heraldry — the shield is preserved exactly as in the input image; the dark blue / muted steel-blue knee-length trousers slightly loose at the cuff with soft painted vertical fold lines; the chunky warm-brown leather boots reaching mid-calf with a darker folded ankle cuff at the top and wide flat soles. ===== POSE (must match the input image exactly) ===== Three-quarter view, body angled approximately fifteen to twenty-five degrees to the viewer's left so the character's anatomical right side faces camera, weight planted evenly on both feet with a very slight forward lean. THE WEAPON ARM (anatomical-RIGHT, on the viewer-LEFT side of the frame) is extended forward and slightly DOWNWARD, elbow bent at roughly 120 degrees. The hand grips the leather grip of the new sword just below the cross-guard; the cross-guard sits just above the hand at the level of the upper coif; the BLADE extends downward and toward the viewer-LEFT with its tip near hip height angled down — filling the same silhouette envelope the mace head occupied in the input image. The knuckles of the gripping hand are visible to camera. THE SHIELD ARM (anatomical-LEFT, viewer-RIGHT) is bent at the elbow, with the forearm angled across the body bringing the round wooden shield up to upper-chest / shoulder height. The shield's flat face presents toward the viewer at a slight three-quarter angle. The hand and forearm are concealed BEHIND the shield. The pose reads as a calm guard stance — sword ready but not raised, shield up but not braced. The pose is NOT a swing, NOT a thrust, NOT a charge — both arms are static and balanced. ===== STYLE / OUTLINE ===== Supercell / Kingshot stylized mobile game art, hand-painted digital illustration with soft painted shading and visible brushwork, warm subsurface skin, saturated rich colors, warm shadow tones never gray, soft painted highlights on metal and leather. Clean thin black silhouette outline around the entire character in deep warm brown-black with even weight, slight thickening in shadowed recesses, slight thinning where forms catch the key light. Interior linework remains soft and value-driven, never hard black. ===== FRAMING / SCALE (must match the input image exactly) ===== 1024x1024 square canvas. Character occupies approximately 66.8% of canvas vertical height (~684px tall) and approximately 60.8% of canvas horizontal width (~623px wide, including the new sword and shield extending the silhouette envelope). TOP of mail coif at approximately 16.6% from top of canvas (~170px headroom). BOTTOM of boots at approximately 16.6% from bottom of canvas (~170px below). Character vertically centered, NOT bottom-anchored. Approximately 18.3% empty margin viewer-LEFT, approximately 20.9% empty margin viewer-RIGHT. DO NOT zoom in, DO NOT shrink, DO NOT add wider borders, DO NOT crop the head or feet, DO NOT shift to bottom edge. Clean white background with no environmental context, no ground plane, no scenery, minimal contact shadow, 1:1 square aspect ratio. ===== NEGATIVE — DO NOT ===== DO NOT keep any part of the spiked wooden mace — the mace is FULLY REPLACED by the new sword; DO NOT render the new weapon as a mace, club, hammer, axe, or anything other than a straight arming sword; DO NOT add iron spikes anywhere on the new weapon — the sword has NO spikes; DO NOT add a wooden mace head or bulbous head; DO NOT make the sword curved, scimitar-shaped, falchion-shaped, kukri-shaped, or any non-straight blade — straight double-edged blade only; DO NOT make the sword two-handed (greatsword, claymore, longsword) — single-handed arming sword only; DO NOT add a basket hilt, swept hilt, or any complex guard — simple straight cross-guard only; DO NOT make the cross-guard ornate, gilded, jeweled, or filigreed — plain steel cross-guard; DO NOT add a complex pommel (eagle-head, lion-head, jeweled) — plain disc pommel only; DO NOT make the blade gold, brass, or bronze — cool silver-gray steel only; DO NOT add gold trim, gold pommel, gold cross-guard, or any gold anywhere on the sword; DO NOT make the grip wrap any color other than warm-brown leather (matching the existing bracers); DO NOT add a sheath or scabbard — sword is held bare in the hand; DO NOT add a baldric, sword belt, or hip strap supporting the sword (the sword is being held, not worn); DO NOT change any facial feature including the jawline, brow shape, eye color, eye shape, nose shape, mouth shape, mustache shape, beard shape, beard length, or skin tone; DO NOT grow the beard into a wild bushy beard or extend it past the upper neck; DO NOT remove the beard or trim it to clean-shaven; DO NOT change the hair color or hair fringe under the coif; DO NOT remove or modify the mail coif; DO NOT change the livery tunic colors, panel layout, neckline, or hem; DO NOT add scale-mail short sleeves under the new weapon arm — the upper arm remains bare bicep at this level; DO NOT change the rope-cord belt to a leather belt — the belt remains rope cord at this level; DO NOT modify the round wooden shield in any way — the shield is preserved exactly as in the input; DO NOT add iron rivets to the shield rim — those arrive at in the standard chain; DO NOT change the round shield to a heater shield, kite shield, tower shield, or any non-round shape; DO NOT change the trouser color (dark blue / muted steel-blue) or boot color (warm brown); DO NOT swap the hand holding the new weapon — sword stays in the anatomical-RIGHT hand; DO NOT swap the hand holding the shield; DO NOT change the chibi proportions or head-to-body ratio; DO NOT change the camera angle, framing, or composition; DO NOT crop the figure (especially do not crop the new sword tip if it extends downward); DO NOT change the background, add scenery, add a ground plane, or add cast shadows; DO NOT make the background transparent, checkerboard-patterned, or non-white — the background must be clean opaque white; DO NOT drift the art style toward anime, photorealism, fully painterly, flat shading, cell shading, or pixel art; DO NOT add a glow, rim light, blue rim light, cyan rim light, or any new light source; DO NOT change the aspect ratio from 1:1 square.
